text: Hello and welcome to Real Easy English.
zh: 大家好,欢迎收听Real Easy English。

text: In this podcast we have real conversations in easy English to help you learn.
zh: 在这个播客中,我们用简单的英语进行真实对话,帮助大家学习。

text: I'm Georgie. And I'm Beth. You can find a video of this podcast
zh: 我是Georgie。我是Beth。你可以在我们的网站上找到这个播客的视频

text: and a worksheet to help you learn on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 和帮助你学习的练习单,网址是bbclearningenglish.com。

text: Hi Beth, how are you today? I'm good, thank you. I am very busy.
zh: 嗨,Beth,你今天怎么样?我很好,谢谢。我非常忙。

text: I have a lot of meetings. How are you? I'm okay, thank you, Beth.
zh: 我有很多会要开。你呢?我还好,谢谢,Beth。

text: I don't have many meetings today, but today we are talking about meetings, in particular work meetings,
zh: 我今天没有很多会议,不过今天我们要聊的话题是会议,尤其是工作会议,

text: where you talk to colleagues and discuss ideas or make decisions.
zh: 就是你和同事交流、讨论想法或做决定的那种。

text: That's right. Okay, shall we get started? Yes.
zh: 没错。那我们开始吧?好。

text: So Beth, you have a lot of meetings today. Do you generally have a lot of meetings at work?
zh: 那Beth,你今天有很多会议。你平时工作中经常开很多会吗?

text: It really depends on what I'm working on.
zh: 这其实要看我在做什么项目。

text: So if I have a project where
zh: 如果我有一个项目,

text: I need to meet a lot of people, then I might have quite a few meetings.
zh: 需要见很多人,那我可能会有不少会议。

text: And that's why I'm busy now. But Generally, we have a lot of,
zh: 这也是我现在忙的原因。但一般来说,我们

text: a few meetings together as a team, but I don't think we have loads of meetings.
zh: 作为一个团队会一起开几次会,但我觉得我们不会有特别多的会议。

text: Compared to other jobs, I don't think we have many meetings.
zh: 和其他工作比起来,我觉得我们的会议不算多。

text: Most of our work is producing things and editing and writing.
zh: 我们大部分的工作是制作内容、编辑和写作。

text: But you're right, when there are lots of big projects that we need to talk to other people on the project, we have more meetings.
zh: 不过你说得对,当有很多大项目需要和项目组的其他人沟通时,我们的会议就会多一些。

text: But yeah, like you, it really depends. So when do you think meetings are useful?
zh: 但和你一样,这真的要看情况。那你觉得什么时候开会是有用的?

text: I think when you need to brainstorm.
zh: 我觉得需要头脑风暴的时候。

text: So brainstorm is when you have lots of new ideas and you need to talk about them together.
zh: 头脑风暴就是当你有很多新想法,需要大家一起讨论的时候。

text: I think then meetings are really useful because you You hear lots of new ideas and
zh: 我觉得这种时候开会特别有用,因为你能听到很多新想法,

text: when you're in a room with other people, if, for example, I have an idea and you say, that's good.
zh: 而且当你和其他人在同一个房间里时,比如说我有一个想法,你说,这个不错。

text: And we can also do this and we can all work together.
zh: 我们还可以这样做,大家一起合作。

text: And I think those kind of meetings are really useful.
zh: 我觉得这种会议是很有用的。

text: Yeah, I think that way of doing it is also more efficient.
zh: 是的,我觉得这种方式也更高效。

text: So sometimes it's better to just have one hour, come up with ideas and decide on the ideas.
zh: 所以有时候最好就是花一个小时,想出点子并决定采用哪些。

text: Yeah, definitely. Do you tend to speak up in meetings?
zh: 是的,绝对是这样。你在开会时会主动发言吗?

text: So that means do you ask questions and feel confident to speak?
zh: 也就是说,你会不会提问,并有信心表达自己的想法?

text: I think if the meeting has lots of people, I find it a bit more stressful to speak up
zh: 我觉得如果会议里人很多,我发言会觉得压力比较大,

text: because I get a bit nervous to speak in front of lots of people but if it's a meeting with just our team for
zh: 因为在很多人面前说话我会有点紧张,但如果只是我们团队内部的会议,比如

text: example you guys I like and trust I don't mind speaking up that much.
zh: 你们这些我喜欢又信任的人,我就不太介意主动发言。

text: And sometimes we have meetings in person
zh: 而且有时候我们是面对面开会,

text: sometimes you have meetings on a call so a video call call.
zh: 有时候是通过电话开会,就是视频通话那种。

text: Do you prefer meeting in person or having calls?
zh: 你更喜欢面对面开会还是打电话开会?

text: I think I prefer meetings in person because
zh: 我觉得我更喜欢面对面开会,因为

text: you can kind of see the facial expressions and body language more easily, I think, and it's easier to
zh: 这样能更容易看到对方的表情和肢体语言,而且更容易

text: know when a person wants to speak, these kinds of things. Yeah, no, I agree with what you're saying.
zh: 知道一个人什么时候想发言,诸如此类的事情。是的,我同意你说的。

text: A lot of the meetings that I have had recently have been with people in different time zones in different countries.
zh: 我最近开的很多会议,对方都在不同时区、不同国家。

text: So definitely then a call is better
zh: 所以在这种情况下,视频通话肯定更好,

text: because it's the best way to speak without having to go to different countries.
zh: 因为这是不用亲自跑到别的国家就能交流的最佳方式。

text: But I agree, I think it's nice to be able to
zh: 但我同意,我觉得在面对面会议中

text: see body language better in a face to face meeting.
zh: 能更好地看到肢体语言,这一点很不错。

text: But meetings on calls now are so normal, that there's not loads of difference.
zh: 不过现在视频会议已经太普遍了,其实差别也不算太大。

text: It is also really nice to be able to take calls in your own house.
zh: 而且能在自己家里接会议电话也真的很不错。

text: So working from home home can be really nice because you can have your snacks and drinks nearby.
zh: 所以在家工作可以很惬意,因为你可以随时吃零食、喝饮料。

text: So yeah, they both have their pros and cons. That's true.
zh: 所以,两者各有利弊。确实如此。

text: Okay, let's recap the language we heard during the conversation,
zh: 好了,让我们回顾一下对话中出现的表达,

text: starting with brainstorm, which is when you get together with a group of people to come up with ideas.
zh: 首先是brainstorm,意思是一群人聚在一起想点子。

text: If you speak up in a meeting, you express your opinion using your voice.
zh: 如果你在会议中speak up,就是说出自己的意见。

text: In an in -person meeting, everyone is in the same physical place.
zh: 在in-person meeting(面对面会议)中,大家都在同一个物理空间。

text: We can also call this a face -to -face meeting. And we also talked about having a call.
zh: 我们也可以把这种会议称为face-to-face meeting。我们还谈到了打电话,call。

text: This could be a phone call or a video call.
zh: 这可能是电话通话,也可能是视频通话。

text: And we can also describe this as a remote meeting.
zh: 我们也可以把这种会议叫做remote meeting(远程会议)。

text: That's it for this episode of Real Easy English.
zh: 这就是本期Real Easy English的全部内容。

text: Why not test what you've learned using the worksheet on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 不妨用我们网站bbclearningenglish.com上的练习单来检验一下你学到的内容。

text: And we'll be back next week with another conversation in Easy English. See you then. Goodbye.
zh: 我们下周将带来另一场简单英语对话,到时候见。再见。
